findings.summary: infrastructure/steganography/ is an isolated island with no other infrastructure module importing it
evidence: No other infrastructure module imports from infrastructure.steganography; it is a specialized domain feature in a generic infrastructure layer
findings.suggestion: Move to infrastructure/publishing/steganography/ to make the optional publishing boundary explicit.

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting has both *_reporter.py and *_generator.py naming patterns for similar output-producing modules
evidence: coverage_reporter.py, pipeline_reporter.py use _reporter suffix; test_summary_generator.py, multi_project_reporter.py use _reporter and _generator interchangeably for the same concern
findings.suggestion: Standardize on _reporter.py for all report-generating modules in infrastructure/reporting/.

findings.summary: 174 occurrences of bare except Exception across 57 modules with inconsistent handling patterns
evidence: generator.py line 444: broad except for warmup failure; health_check.py line 55: broad except for check resilience — some documented intentional, many not
findings.suggestion: Narrow broad except-Exception clauses to specific exception types where possible; document intentional broad catches with BLE001 noqa comments.

findings.summary: optimizer.py wraps infrastructure.core.logging_utils in a try/except ImportError guard that is never needed
evidence: optimizer.py:47-53: try/except ImportError guard around get_logger with LOGGING_AVAILABLE flag; every log call then guarded by if LOGGING_AVAILABLE and logger
findings.suggestion: Remove the try/except guard and LOGGING_AVAILABLE flag; import get_logger unconditionally.

findings.summary: dashboard_generator.py guards matplotlib but defers plotly import inside a function with no module-level sentinel
evidence: dashboard_generator.py:963: 'import plotly.graph_objects as go' deferred inside function body with no PLOTLY_AVAILABLE guard
findings.suggestion: Add try/except ImportError for plotly at module level with a PLOTLY_AVAILABLE sentinel matching the matplotlib pattern.

findings.summary: test_coverage_parser.py directly imports and tests private helper functions with leading underscores
findings.suggestion: Test only extract_failed_tests() with varied inputs covering each strategy's case.

findings.summary: Files use typing.List/Dict/Optional despite from __future__ import annotations everywhere making them unnecessary
evidence: environment.py line 43: 'from typing import List, Tuple' while from __future__ import annotations is on line 36
findings.suggestion: Replace List[x], Dict[k,v], Optional[x] with builtin equivalents across infrastructure files.

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting has 13 modules with overlapping coverage/test concerns making import surface opaque
evidence: 01_run_tests.py lines 31-41: imports from coverage_reporter, coverage_parser, and test_runner separately for the same reporting concern
findings.suggestion: Consolidate coverage_reporter, coverage_parser into two modules: parsing (input side) and formatting/saving (output side).

findings.summary: generate_test_report reads both infrastructure and project coverage from the same htmlcov/coverage.json path
evidence: Lines 261-268: infra_coverage_json and project_coverage_json both point to repo_root / 'htmlcov' / 'coverage.json'
findings.suggestion: Use coverage_infra.json for infrastructure and coverage_project.json for project coverage paths.

findings.summary: run_infrastructure_tests and run_project_tests share ~70% identical structure with minimal variation
evidence: Lines 83-174 and 177-274: both functions do identical sequence of start_time, clean, get config, build cmd, setup env, discover, run TestSuiteConfig
findings.suggestion: Extract _build_test_command() and _run_suite() helpers parametrized by suite type.

findings.summary: steganography/core.py uses deprecated tempfile.mktemp() which has a race condition
evidence: Line 141: tmp = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.pdf', prefix='steg_')) — mktemp is deprecated
findings.suggestion: Replace with: fd, tmp_str = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix='.pdf', prefix='steg_'); os.close(fd); tmp = Path(tmp_str)

findings.summary: optimizer.py LOGGING_AVAILABLE boolean guard pattern is not used anywhere else in the codebase
evidence: Lines 47-53 and 269,278,285,299,304: conditional logging pattern unique to this file; all other modules use unconditional get_logger
findings.suggestion: Remove LOGGING_AVAILABLE guard. Use standard logger = get_logger(__name__) at module level.
